project_type: Python Telegram bot for OMR (Optical Mark Recognition) test grading
build_system: pip with requirements.txt, deploy.sh for systemd service
test_framework: No formal test suite - validation is done by running import checks and generating sheets
build_command: cd /projects/sandbox/ufqbot/test_grader_bot && python -c 'import bot; import omr_scanner; import sheet_generator; import excel_export; import config; print("OK")'
test_command: cd /projects/sandbox/ufqbot/test_grader_bot && python -c 'from sheet_generator import generate_answer_sheet; from omr_scanner import scan_answer_sheet; print("Imports OK")'
verification_instructions: 1. Run import checks for all modules. 2. Generate a test sheet and verify it creates a valid PNG. 3. Verify the multi-method scanner logic is syntactically correct and importable. 4. Verify shared test storage creates/reads JSON files in data/tests/.
setup_instructions: pip install opencv-python-headless Pillow openpyxl numpy python-dotenv python-telegram-bot
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. Python 3.11 available via pyenv. No Docker needed - direct Python execution. Must stay under 120MB RAM at runtime. Target server: 2GB RAM AWS free tier with ~300MB already used by 2 other bots.
contribution_requirements: All user-facing text must be in Uzbek. No new pip dependencies beyond what is in requirements.txt. JSON file storage only.
key_patterns: Single-file modules: config.py (settings), bot.py (telegram handlers), omr_scanner.py (CV processing), sheet_generator.py (Pillow image generation), excel_export.py (openpyxl). Data stored in data/ directory as JSON. Session-based state via JSON files. python-telegram-bot ConversationHandler pattern.
directory_structure: test_grader_bot/ - main bot directory. test_grader_bot/data/ - runtime data storage (sessions, tests). Sheet layout constants shared between sheet_generator.py and omr_scanner.py.
